scope.context_note: Volume III section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Bounds', section 'Bounds Algebra'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Subset Inclusion Preserves Upper Bounds
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $u$ is an upper bound of $B$, then $u$ is an upper bound of $A$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $u$ is an upper bound of $B$, then $u$ is an upper bound of $A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

nodes.title: Every Element Lies Below the Supremum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. Then $x\le s$ for every $x\in A$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. Then $x\le s$ for every $x\in A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

nodes.title: Maximum Implies Supremum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $m\in A$. If $m$ is a maximum of $A$, then $m=\sup A$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $m\in A$. If $m$ is a maximum of $A$, then $m=\sup A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Maximum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set and let $A \subseteq S$. An element $M \in S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M \in A$ and $M$ is an upper bound of $A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Maximum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set and let $A \subseteq S$. An element $M \in S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M \in A$ and $M$ is an upper bound of $A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

ancestor_tree.title: Supremum
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. nodes.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq S\subseteq S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq S\subseteq S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

nodes.title: Supremum in the Set Is the Maximum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $s=\sup A$. If $s\in A$, then $s$ is a maximum of $A$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $s=\sup A$. If $s\in A$, then $s$ is a maximum of $A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Maximum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set and let $A \subseteq S$. An element $M \in S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M \in A$ and $M$ is an upper bound of $A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Maximum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set and let $A \subseteq S$. An element $M \in S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M \in A$ and $M$ is an upper bound of $A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Uniqueness of the Maximum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $m_1$ and $m_2$ are both maximum elements of $A$, then $m_1=m_2$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $m_1$ and $m_2$ are both maximum elements of $A$, then $m_1=m_2$.

direct_dependencies.title: Maximum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set and let $A \subseteq S$. An element $M \in S$ is the maximum of $A$ if and only if $M \in A$ and $M$ is an upper bound of $A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Uniqueness of the Supremum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $s_1$ and $s_2$ are both suprema of $A$, then $s_1=s_2$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$. If $s_1$ and $s_2$ are both suprema of $A$, then $s_1=s_2$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

nodes.title: Upper Bound Comparison with the Supremum
nodes.statement_display: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. For $u\in\mathbb{R}$, the number $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ if and only if $s\le u$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. For $u\in\mathbb{R}$, the number $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ if and only if $s\le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

ancestor_tree.title: Upper Bound
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Order Comparison of Suprema
nodes.statement_display: Let $A,B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above. If for every $x\in A$ there exists $y\in B$ such that $x\le y$, then $\sup A\le \sup B$.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $A,B\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above. If for every $x\in A$ there exists $y\in B$ such that $x\le y$, then $\sup A\le \sup B$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Every Element Lies Below the Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. Then $x\le s$ for every $x\in A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound Comparison with the Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. For $u\in\mathbb{R}$, the number $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ if and only if $s\le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Language
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Every Element Lies Below the Supremum
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. Then $x\le s$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

ancestor_tree.title: Upper Bound Comparison with the Supremum
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ be nonempty and bounded above, and let $s=\sup A$. For $u\in\mathbb{R}$, the number $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ if and only if $s\le u$.

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Upper Bounds Depend on the Ambient Order
nodes.statement_display: Let $(S,\le_S)$ and $(T,\le_T)$ be ordered sets, and let $A\subseteq S\cap T$. Whether $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ must be interpreted relative to the chosen ambient order.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $(S,\le_S)$ and $(T,\le_T)$ be ordered sets, and let $A\subseteq S\cap T$. Whether $u$ is an upper bound of $A$ must be interpreted relative to the chosen ambient order.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Upper Bound
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

nodes.title: Ambient Existence of Supremum
nodes.statement_display: There are ordered sets $S\subseteq S'$ and a subset $A\subseteq S$ such that $A$ has a supremum relative to $S'$ but has no supremum relative to $S$.
nodes.statement_tex: There are ordered sets $S\subseteq S'$ and a subset $A\subseteq S$ such that $A$ has a supremum relative to $S'$ but has no supremum relative to $S$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq S\subseteq S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Variable
dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Partial Order
dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

ancestor_tree.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq S\subseteq S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.

dependencies.title: Supremum

dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Bound

nodes.title: Rational Gap Example for Suprema
nodes.statement_display:
Let
\[
  A=\{q\in\mathbb{Q}:q^2<2\}.
\]
Then $A$ is nonempty and bounded above in $\mathbb{Q}$, but $A$ has no supremum relative to $\mathbb{Q}$.

\]
Then $A$ is nonempty and bounded above in $\mathbb{Q}$, but $A$ has no supremum relative to $\mathbb{Q}$.

direct_dependencies.title: Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

direct_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

direct_dependencies.title: Ambient Existence of Supremum
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: There are ordered sets $S\subseteq S'$ and a subset $A\subseteq S$ such that $A$ has a supremum relative to $S'$ but has no supremum relative to $S$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Suprema Depend on the Ambient Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A\subseteq S\subseteq S'$ be subsets of an ordered set. The supremum of $A$ relative to $S$, if it exists, need not agree with the supremum of $A$ relative to $S'$.
